title: Temple of Light: Avatar Puzzle Challenge
description: The player controls a ReadyPlayerMe avatar navigating through a mystical godrays temple, activating ancient light pillars in the correct sequence to unlock a sealed portal. Each pillar emits a unique color when activated, and the player must memorize and repeat a randomized 5-step light pattern while avoiding shadow zones that drain energy.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements an interactive 3D puzzle game. Load both GLB assets using GLTFLoader: place the godrays_demo scene as the environment representing an ancient temple with volumetric light shafts, and load the readyplayer.me model as the player avatar standing on the temple floor. Set up a third-person camera that follows the avatar from behind at a fixed offset of (0, 3, -5), using OrbitControls with limited vertical rotation. Add ambient light at intensity 0.3 and a warm directional light at intensity 1.2 casting shadows. Create 5 glowing pillar meshes (CylinderGeometry) arranged in a semicircle in front of the temple, each with a unique emissive color (red, blue, green, yellow, purple). Implement WASD keyboard controls to move the avatar with basic animation blending if the model has animations, otherwise translate the model smoothly at speed 3 units per second. When the game starts, display a UI overlay showing 'Watch the pattern' and play a random sequence of 5 pillar activations where each pillar scales up briefly and glows brighter for 0.8 seconds with 0.5 second gaps. After the sequence plays, show 'Your turn' and let the player walk the avatar to each pillar. Detect proximity within 1.5 units to activate a pillar via raycasting or distance check. Track the player's activation order and validate each step: correct activations flash green on a HUD progress bar, incorrect ones flash red and reset the attempt with a new pattern. Add 3 shadow zone planes on the ground with dark animated ShaderMaterial that pulse slowly. If the avatar steps on a shadow zone, decrease an energy bar by 20 percent shown in the top-right corner. Display a win screen when all 5 pillars are activated correctly and a lose screen if energy reaches zero. Include a restart button on both end screens.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  avatarPositionX: number,         // X coordinate of the player avatar in world space
  avatarPositionZ: number,         // Z coordinate of the player avatar in world space
  activePillarIndex: number,         // Index (0-4) of the currently glowing pillar, -1 if none
  sequenceStep: number,         // Current step in the pattern sequence (0-4 during playback/input)
  playerInputCount: number,         // Number of pillars the player has activated in current attempt
  isShowingPattern: boolean,         // True when the game is displaying the pattern to memorize
  isPlayerTurn: boolean,         // True when waiting for player input
  correctMatches: number,         // Count of correct pillar activations in current attempt
  gamePhase: string,         // Current game state - 'intro', 'showing', 'input', 'success', 'failure'
  portalUnlocked: boolean,         // True when the player completes the sequence correctly
  totalAttempts: number,         // Number of times the player has attempted the puzzle
  patternLength: number,         // Length of the sequence to memorize (always 5)
}
